mechanism_narrative: An always-on rising federal funds rate trend raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, so higher recent FEDFUNDS momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered higher FEDFUNDS momentum observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downside crossing in retail sales signals weakening household demand and lower expected corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS crosses below its 6-month moving average after being above it in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS downside crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances or the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rises while policy rates are elevated, labor weakness converts monetary restraint into recession risk, so the joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS and UNRATE trigger has a nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 return or if its downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An always-on positive industrial production trend indicates improving real output and operating leverage, so stronger INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum does not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO momentum in walk-forward tests or if rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with stable or falling unemployment signals income support without labor-market stress, raising expected earnings and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS and UNRATE condition is not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered observations or has mean returns below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An upside crossing in CPI inflation while the policy rate is high signals renewed tightening pressure and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its 12-month growth rate while FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration with FEDFUNDS above 4.0% is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances or the triggered mean return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A downside crossing in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, so HOUST deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average after being above it in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if downside HOUST crossings have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered cases or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: An always-on positive real GDP trend indicates broad demand growth and higher aggregate earnings, so stronger GDPC1 momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 momentum fails to outperform negative GDPC1 momentum for 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests or if rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03.
